name: Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón Trail (PR-A 91)
name_original: Castillo de Turón - Fuente Garzón – circular desde Ardales (PR-A 91)
description: Hike the moderate 8.3-mile Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón Trail (PR-A 91) near Ardales, featuring a Roman bridge, castle ruins, and panoramic v
region_intro: Hiking around Ardales offers diverse landscapes within Málaga province, characterized by the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Natural Park and the scenic Guadalhorce Reservoirs. The region features impressive mountain landscapes, deep canyons, and significant geological formations. Trails often wind through oak and pine trees, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Ardales The most popular hiking route is Gaitanejo Trail, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 15 minutes to complete, offering views of the Conde de Guadalhorce Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Caminito del Rey – Caminito del Rey loop from Puerto de las Atalayas, a difficult 9.8 miles (15.8 km) path. This route navigates the vertical walls of the Gaitanes Gorge, providing views of the landscape and turquoise waters. Local hikers also love the Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón Trail (PR-A 91), an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Ardales is defined by dramatic gorges, scenic reservoirs, and trails through oak and pine forests. The network offers options for easy family-friendly walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Ardales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Ardales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ardales?
A: Ardales offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 10 easy, 25 moderate, and 7 difficult options to explore the region's stunning landscapes.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Ardales?
A: The mild temperatures in winter make it an ideal time for exploration, offering comfortable conditions for hiking. Spring and autumn also provide pleasant weather, while summer can be quite warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Ardales?
A: Yes, Ardales has several family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995634479/gaitanejo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaitanejo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy and well-marked path that winds through pine trees, offering beautiful views of the Conde de Guadalhorce Reservoir. It's a great choice for families looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Ardales?
A: Ardales is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You'll encounter the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Natural Park, the scenic Guadalhorce Reservoirs, and trails winding through oak and pine forests. Notable attractions include the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995899160/cerro-del-calvario-capellan-turon-circular-from-ardales-pr-a-90" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Turón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Ardales?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995899160/cerro-del-calvario-capellan-turon-circular-from-ardales-pr-a-90" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario - Capellán - Turón circular from Ardales (PR-A 90)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.3-mile (13.3 km) path that allows you to return to your starting point after exploring the area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ardales?
A: The hiking routes in Ardales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the sense of 'total disconnection' amidst oaks and pine trees, and the stunning views of the reservoirs and gorges.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Ardales offers challenging routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/431048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey – Caminito del Rey loop from Puerto de las Atalayas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.8-mile (15.8 km) path that navigates the vertical walls of the Gaitanes Gorge, providing awe-inspiring views and an exhilarating experience.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Guadalhorce Reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The Guadalhorce Reservoirs are a prominent feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995634479/gaitanejo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaitanejo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers beautiful views of the Conde de Guadalhorce Reservoir, showcasing the stunning contrast between water, green trees, and blue sky.

Q: Are there any historical sites or caves accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Ardales has a rich history. While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17224850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mountains – Cave Houses of Sierra Parda loop&lt;/a&gt; offers glimpses of ancient dwellings, the region is also home to the Cueva de Ardales, an archaeological gem with cave paintings. Access to the cave interior typically requires booking a guided tour, but the surrounding trails provide historical context.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Ardales?
A: The Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Natural Park and the nearby Parque Natural de la Sierra de las Nieves are vital parts of the region's ecosystem. Hikers might spot diverse flora, including the majestic Spanish fir, and if lucky, the elusive Iberian ibex, especially in the more remote mountain areas.

Q: Are there opportunities for water sports or wild swimming near the hiking areas?
A: While the focus of the trails is hiking, the Guadalhorce Reservoirs not only provide picturesque scenery but also opportunities for water sports. Although specific wild swimming spots are not always marked on hiking trails, the presence of the reservoirs suggests possibilities for enjoying the water in designated areas.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with panoramic scenery?
A: Many trails in Ardales offer rewarding viewpoints. The hike to the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995899160/cerro-del-calvario-capellan-turon-circular-from-ardales-pr-a-90" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Turón&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of Ardales and its surroundings. Additionally, routes through the Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Natural Park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/431048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer awe-inspiring vistas of the gorge and turquoise waters from elevated walkways.
tips: Ardales enjoys a strategic location thanks to its proximity to the Guadalhorce reservoirs and the Sierra de las Nieves. The highlight of the town is the castle of La Peña, which dates from the 9th century and is located on a promontory 496 meters high.

Here is a bridge over the Turón River dating from the 1st century BC. C. Although it only conserves three of its five original arches, it is one of the few Roman bridges that still allows the passage of vehicles and pedestrians today.

The castle originally had more than ten watchtowers that served as support for the castle of Ardales during the Nasrid period. What remains of the wall was built on the limestone cliff, which made the castle a strategic and impregnable point.

faqs.question: Where does the Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón Trail (PR-A 91) start?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins at "la Esquina los Herreros" in Ardales. As it's a circular route, you'll return to this same starting point.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: Most hikers complete the 13.3 km (8.3 miles) route in about 4 hours, though your pace and stops for views or photos will influence this.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the PR-A 91?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start on village streets, transition to a comfortable wide track along the Turón River, then move into Mediterranean scrubland and dense pine forests. There are gentle ascents and descents, with a steeper section leading down from Cerro del Rey.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest, including the historic La Molina Roman Bridge, the ruins of Turón Castle, and the Castillo de la Peña de Ardales. The trail also offers panoramic views of nearby reservoirs and Ardales from higher points like Cerro del Rey.

faqs.question: Is the Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón Trail (PR-A 91) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, please keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in sections where wildlife might be present. Always carry bags to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are ideal. The weather is milder, and the landscape is vibrant with wildflowers in spring or rich with autumn colors. Summers can be very hot, making early morning hikes essential if visiting then.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón Trail (PR-A 91). It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ardales offers parking options. Since the trail starts at "la Esquina los Herreros" within the village, you can typically find street parking or designated parking areas nearby. Arriving earlier in the day, especially on weekends, is advisable to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly links up with sections of the GR-7 path (specifically the Ardales - Serrato segment) and also covers parts of the official "Los Jiménez - Fuente Garzón" trail itself, as well as "Andalucía (Provincia de Málaga)" and "Cerro del Calvario - Capellán - Turón" trails.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: As you transition from agricultural land to Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests, you might spot various bird species, small mammals, and reptiles. The flora includes olive and almond trees, Mediterranean scrub, and dense pine forests, with occasional Spanish firs at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, the trail is manageable for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. The varied terrain and some ascents provide a good challenge without being overly strenuous. Take your time and enjoy the diverse scenery.

waypoints.name: Castillo de la Peña de Ardales
